reasoning_trace.signal: No products found. Premium D2C presence absent.

reasoning_trace.tension: Absent while Myntra and Meesho show strong presence; suggests demand is price-driven, not style-driven.

reasoning_trace.signal: One product at rank 3, 65% discount, 3.2 rating, 2500 reviews, review velocity 180 (adjusted 45). Discount context: Subsidized Liquidation.
reasoning_trace.proves: The product has high visibility and volume, but demand is driven by extreme discount, not style preference.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove genuine style demand; the discount is the primary purchase driver.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong presence but heavily distorted by discount; conflicts with any notion of organic demand.

reasoning_trace.proves: Strong volume in price-sensitive segment; indicates demand at low price point.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand at higher price points or style-driven preference; price is key.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong volume but low price and moderate rating; aligns with Myntra's discount-driven signal but conflicts with Nykaa's absence.

reasoning_trace.proves: No internal evidence to confirm or contradict external signals.

reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Both Myntra and Meesho show strong volume at deep discount/low price, suggesting price sensitivity dominates.

eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent while Myntra and Meesho show strong but discount-driven demand

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong presence
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present in premium D2C.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong presence but demand is subsidized liquidation (65% off, 3.2 rating).
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand real or purely price-driven?
conflicts.proves: The style has traction only at deep discount or low price points.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has genuine appeal at higher price points or without heavy discount.

convergences.title: Price sensitivity dominates
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (65% off) and Meesho (₹299) show demand only when price is extremely low.
convergences.proves: The target customer is highly price-sensitive.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove style preference or repeat purchase intent.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: High volume at low price points suggests there is a market for this style among budget-conscious buyers. Meesho's 8000+ units sold indicates willingness to buy at ₹299.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: Demand is entirely price-driven; no premium signal, heavy discount distortion on Myntra, and moderate ratings. Without deep discounting, this style may not sell.

nykaa.reason: Absence is clear; no products found.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by Subsidized Liquidation; review velocity down-weighted 75%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume with moderate rating; no noise flags.

internal.reason: No data available; cannot confirm or deny.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: Only two sources show signal, both price-driven. No premium or organic demand evidence. A small trial at low price points can validate if the style has any repeat purchase potential.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Will the style sell at a price above ₹500 without heavy discount?
watch_triggers: Nykaa adds products at full price
Myntra discount drops below 40% with stable rank
Competitor Meta ads appear for this style
eval-discount-trap.demand_integrity: Subsidized Liquidation on Myntra; price-driven on Meesho. Overall demand integrity is low.

eval-premium-mirage.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand confirmed at Nykaa/Myntra but Meesho absent — trend may be niche, not mass

conflicts.title: Nykaa/Myntra show demand but Meesho absent
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't work for value-fashion customers?
conflicts.proves: Premium demand exists at full price and slight discount
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether demand is broad enough for mass retail or value channels

convergences.title: Clean demand signal at premium price points
convergences.detail: Nykaa shows full-price products with high ratings; Myntra shows strategic value pricing with low discount. Both indicate genuine style preference.
convergences.proves: The style has appeal at higher price bands
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable or scalable

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: Nykaa and Myntra both show clean, low-discount demand for this premium kurti style. The product has strong ratings and strategic pricing, indicating genuine consumer interest at higher price points.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Meesho absence is a major red flag — no demand in the value segment. Without internal POS data or social/ad validation, the trend may be too niche for broad retail. Missing evidence from multiple sources limits confidence.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price products, high ratings

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, low discount, clean demand signal

eval-premium-mirage.bet_rationale: Premium demand is confirmed but limited to two sources; Meesho absence and missing social/ad data suggest narrow appeal. A small trial in premium stores can validate real demand before scaling.
eval-premium-mirage.key_uncertainty: Will this style generate demand in value channels (Meesho) or is it strictly premium?

eval-mass-only.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Meesho strong — trend is value-driven, not premium.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Meesho strong: premium vs value divide
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; zero products at ₹800+.
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at discount; price sensitivity present.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong demand at ₹199; reseller growth accelerating.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this trend viable only at low price points, or can it be positioned higher?
conflicts.proves: Demand exists in value-fashion channels; premium channel shows no interest.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the style has aspirational appeal or is purely commodity-driven.

convergences.title: Mass commodity demand confirmed
convergences.detail: Meesho (52k units) and Myntra (900 reviews) both show demand for basic cotton kurti at low prices.
convergences.proves: Style has broad appeal in price-sensitive segments.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is sustainable without deep discounts.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume (52k+ units) with accelerating reseller growth, indicating robust demand in tier-2/3/4 markets. Myntra also shows moderate demand. The style is a proven mass commodity.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa's absence signals no premium demand; Myntra's discount risk suggests price-driven purchasing. Internal POS has no data, and social/ad signals are missing, leaving demand integrity unclear. The trend may be limited to value-fashion.

nykaa.reason: No data; absence is a strong negative signal for premium channel.

myntra.reason: Moderate presence but discount risk high; review velocity down-weighted 25%.

meesho.reason: Strong volume and reseller growth; clean signal for value segment.

eval-mass-only.bet_rationale: Meesho and Myntra show demand, but Nykaa's absence and discount risk suggest price sensitivity. A small trial in value-fashion channels can validate real demand without overcommitting.
eval-mass-only.key_uncertainty: Is the demand style-driven or purely price-driven?

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium and mass tiers with no discount distortion

convergences.title: Nykaa + Myntra + Meesho all show strong demand
convergences.detail: Nykaa has 2 full-price products at ₹1450 with high ratings; Myntra has rank 2 with 10% discount and 3200 reviews; Meesho has 28,000+ units sold with 450 resellers. All signals are positive and consistent.
convergences.proves: The style has broad appeal from premium to value segments, with genuine demand not driven by heavy discounts.
convergences.cannot_prove: Internal store performance; long-term sustainability; exact price elasticity across tiers.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across all three external sources: premium full-price at Nykaa, high rank with minimal discount at Myntra, and massive volume at Meesho. The reseller growth on Meesho indicates accelerating adoption in tier-2/3 markets.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data exists to validate against your own store performance. YouTube and Meta Ads data are absent, so we cannot assess organic vs paid demand drivers. However, the convergence of three independent sources with clean signals is compelling.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence with full-price products and high ratings; clean signal.

myntra.reason: Rank 2 with low discount and high review velocity; Strategic Value Pricing.

meesho.reason: High volume and accelerating reseller growth; strong demand in value segment.

internal.reason: No prior data; cannot confirm or contradict.

eval-golden-convergence.bet_rationale: All three external sources show strong, clean demand with no discount distortion. The style has proven appeal from premium to mass markets. Recommend widening to multiple stores or regions.
eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: How will the style perform in your specific store demographics without prior internal data?

eval-golden-convergence.demand_integrity: CLEAN — all products are Strategic Value Pricing or full-price; no noise flags detected.

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong premium demand with zero discount, and Myntra confirms moderate demand with clean pricing. This suggests the style has genuine appeal among quality-conscious buyers.
